title: 'Mormon Wives' Star Jessi Ngatikaura's Estranged Husband, Jordan, Files a Restraining Order Against Her
summary: “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star Jessi Draper Ngatikaura is facing a restraining order from estranged husband Jordan Ngatikaura.
story: “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star Jessi Draper Ngatikaura is facing a restraining order from estranged husband Jordan Ngatikaura.
In a court document obtained by NBC News, Jordan Ngatikaura filed for a temporary restraining order on March 20. The reality star filed an opposition the same day.
Jessi Ngatikaura's attorneys declined to comment. Reps for Jordan Ngatikaura did not immediately respond to NBC News' request for comment.
The request came a day after Jordan Ngatikaura filed for divorce, according to a court document obtained by NBC News.

“This has not been an easy decision and it comes with a heavy heart,” Jordan Ngatikaura said in a statement to People following the divorce filing. “I’m grateful for the shared memories and the lessons. While our paths are now moving in different directions, my priority remains my children and ensuring they feel loved, supported, and protected through this transition. I am committed to handling this next chapter with kindness and respect.”
The couple met at a birthday party in 2019, he shared in an episode of the “Weekly Trash” podcast, and got married in 2020.
Jordan Ngatikaura has a daughter, Peyton, from a previous marriage. He and the “Mormon Wives” star, who has also previously been married, share two children, Jovi and Jagger.

The couple’s marital issues have been documented on the Hulu reality show, including infidelity rumors and their 90-day separation. During Season 3, Jessi Ngatikaura revealed she had an “emotional affair” with “Vanderpump Villa” star Marciano Brunette, which led to the couple’s decision to be open and honest on camera about their personal lives.

“When we started talking about it on camera, we both agreed that if we’re going to do this, we’re going to do it 100%. We’re going to own our s---. We’re going to talk about the mistakes we’ve made and take the audience along on the journey,” she told TODAY.com in November 2025.
Now in its fourth season, which was released on March 12, viewers had been curious if the two were still together.

“We still have ups and downs and hard days and, you know, easier days. But that’s just real life,” Jessi Ngatikaura told “Entertainment Tonight” about her relationship with her husband, sharing that she felt it was “important to share” all parts of her life and marriage.

At the time of her interview, she told the outlet, “We’re together. We’ve been back together for almost a year. We’re still working on it. We’re still in therapy. It’s still easy and hard at the same time.”
She also said that she can't easily walk away from her marriage despite their issues because of their children, as well as financial reasons.
When joking about needing to protect her newfound empire,  Jessi Ngatikaura said, “Yeah, prenups get ‘em.”

title: Why Dionne Warwick Hand Picked Teyana Taylor to Play Her in Biopic
summary: Dionne Warwick is gushing over Teyana Taylor, who is set to portray the music icon in an upcoming biopic.
story: Dionne Warwick is gushing over Teyana Taylor, who is set to portray the music icon in an upcoming biopic.

On Monday, March 23, Warwick stopped by TODAY to chat about her new album, “DWuets,” which will be her last. Over the past few weeks, the legendary singer has been sharing details about the record on social media, including that Diane Warren wrote all the tracks and that it features duets with Cynthia Erivo and Kehlani.
While on TODAY, Warwick spoke to Al Roker, Dylan Dreyer and Craig Melvin about her new single, “Ocean in the Desert” with Erivo, before talking about another project she has in the works: a biopic.
Al mentioned that the “I Say a Little Prayer for You” singer “hand-picked” Taylor to play her in the movie.
The singer recalled that her son Damon Elliott sent her a photograph of the “One Battle After Another” star side-by-side with a throwback picture of Warwick.
“I said, ‘Oh my God, it’s not me?’ I thought ‘I’m going to talk to my daddy about this one. Wait a minute,’” Warwick joked.
"Then I met her," she added. "She’s such a personable young lady and very talented. She was very excited about even being thought of to do it.”

The TODAY co-hosts then showed Warwick a clip of Taylor’s sit-down with Craig on TODAY in January where she discussed the role and how she plans to pull it off. At the time, Craig asked if Taylor intends to sing in the film.
“Yeah, but hopefully I’m lip-syncing. Because, baby, Dionne Warwick, that’s a whole different level of voice. I got pipes but she’s got pipes," Taylor responded.
(Later in that interview, Taylor predicted she might go the same route as Angela Bassett in “What’s Love Got to Do With It” and mouth along to Warwick’s recorded vocals.)
Warwick and the co-hosts laughed before the icon said she would support Taylor lip-syncing.
“If that’s what she wants to do,” Warwick said in between giggles. “She is so cute. That’s all I can think of … and she says what she means.”
“That really surprised me," she added. "I thought, ‘Oh God, she is me.’”

Warwick frequently compliments Taylor on social media, recently applauding the actor on X after she earned her first Oscar nomination at the 98th Academy Awards. “So proud of my baby @TEYANATAYLOR on so many levels,” she wrote.
“That’s my girl,” she said, in part. “You go dear!!!”
Taylor has been attached to the Warwick movie for years. She first mentioned they were in the “building process” for the film in April 2023.
During Taylor’s TODAY interview in January 2026, she explained why the film has been in development for years.

“That is my lady. She’s so amazing,” Taylor told Craig. “I’m just so happy to make her proud all while also just being very protective of her, making sure we have the perfect story. She has so much career … I know it’s taken a little while but we just want it to be perfect. And I want it to be perfect for her.”

title: Ariel Sullivan Says ‘Beneath,’ Her Next Book, Is ‘Darker’ Than ‘Conform.’ Read a Preview
summary: Ariel Sullivan is back with another novel. She previews "Beneath," the start of a new trilogy set hundreds of years before the events of "Conform."
story: Six months after her debut novel "Conform" was published, Ariel Sullivan is back with another novel.
"Beneath," out March 24, is the start of a new trilogy that takes place 200 years before the stratified world of "Conform" was created, laying the groundwork for how the dystopia came to be.
Both novels are published through Jenna Bush Hager's Thousand Voices x Random House Publishing Group imprint.

"It's a breathtaking dystopian romance that shows how love can grow even in the darkest places. It's about found family, resilience and what happens when people start to question the rules they've been living by," Jenna said of "Beneath" on TODAY March 23.

"Beneath" takes place about seven years after a world war causes the global order's collapse. Sasha, our main character, is living in an underground society with other survivors. It's a grittier world than Emeline's in "Conform."

"'Conform' and 'Beneath' trilogies are the same coin, different sides. 'Conform' is that shiny side. 'Beneath' is hidden and corroded. It's emotional and character-driven. It's darker. It's grittier. So anything that you expected from 'Conform,' don't expect it in 'Beneath,'" Sullivan tells TODAY.com.
The idea for "Beneath" came when she was writing backstory for "Conform."
"I was working on world-building and writing about why things were the way they were. Sasha showed up. She just stomped into the picture and sat down," she says.

Sullivan says, in a creative burst, she burned the dinner she was making that night. "We ordered pizza, and I drafted those three books, like, very roughly, but like, drafted them out. And I was like, 'This is what has to happen,'" she says.

Keeping with the novel's "darker" themes, Sullivan teases that the novel's core group of characters — Unit Seven, a squadron of elite soldiers that Sasha ends up joining for an Earth-saving mission — each represent one of the seven stages of grief.

"You've got shock and denial and anger and bargaining and acceptance. It'll be interesting if readers can find out which characters are what," Sullivan says, adding that she'll eventually reveal who is who, but not yet.

After "Beneath," Sullivan will release the sequel to "Conform," which will take place right where we left off with Emeline. There's also a third trilogy that Sullivan is waiting to share details about.
But for now, we're diving "Beneath." Check out an excerpt below.
Excerpt from Chapter 15 of 'Beneath'

In the following excerpt, Sasha Cadell is about to step foot onto the Earth’s surface, now a nuclear wasteland, for the first time after living underground for six years. But she’s not alone. Unit Seven, a squadron of elite soldiers, are right behind her, performing one final check of their radiation-protective gear before embarking into unknown terrain in pursuit of Mission Hope — the key to humanity’s survival…
“Audio, Cadell.”
Sweat dripped down my back. “Check.”
“Night vision,” Patrick called out.
Everyone hit buttons on the left arm of their suits. I stared at the series of buttons Levi had given me a rundown on and hit mine. The shield of my helmet shifted to night vision.
“Check.” I clicked it off.
“Navigation systems.”
I pressed the next button, and a map appeared with eight dots showing our location. “Check.”
“Partner communication.”
I hit another button, and Levi’s dry voice filled my head. “Last chance to run, Sasha.”
“I’m not running,” I told him, even as my heart battered against the plates of my gear. He nodded at me, and we switched back to the unit channel. “Check.”
“Oxygen level,” Patrick prompted.
I hit the last button, and my oxygen level projected on the helmet shield. I didn’t know if I would adjust to the helmet. I responded, “Hundred percent.”

“Mission is a go, repeat, mission is a go.” Patrick gave a thumbs-up to Jaxon and the other member of his unit who stood at the shack. I finally looked at the shack itself just once. I hadn’t seen the small hut in almost six years, when I had sprinted to it, demanding care for Lara, barely clinging to life in my arms. I closed my eyes at the memory.

Jaxon hit a button, and the steel door began to close on the shack and all of Haven, the only home I had known since the war. My heart hit my throat as the man called out, “Door closing; Explor­atory Unit Seven is in the holding box.”

Levi tightened the straps of the med bag. Rumi and Patrick danced around each other; whether they talked or simply moved off muscle memory and deep recognition of each other, I didn’t know. Tristian and Ingrid both moved, but it wasn’t coordinated. Multiple times, Tristian’s helmet swiveled in the direction of Levi, his partner of seven years. Guilt settled in my gut at the partnership I had ended. Damien and Isla began executing an elaborate handshake—a fist-pounding, hip-bumping dance that ended in a bow.
Levi’s voice filled my helmet. “Just us. You good?”
“Would it matter if I said no?” I asked, my gloved hands sweaty.
“Yes, it would matter to me,” Levi said, his helmet shield shifting to me until I saw my reflection. Surprise hit me at the formidable force I beheld in his shield.
“Door’s shut. Switching communication to the group channel,” Levi told me.
Tristian cleared his throat, and everyone fell silent. They drifted toward him, forming a circle of sorts. “There’s no one else I’d rather do this with. You know our mission.”

Patrick shifted his weight between the balls of his feet, Rumi si­lent next to him. Isla and Damien bumped lightly into each other. Ingrid was stiff. Levi was a steady presence next to me, the arm of his suit resting against mine. For once I welcomed the contact.
“You ready, Cadell?” Everyone’s helmets were directed at me, and my heart picked up its pace. Could they hear its frantic beating over the communication system?
“Yes,” I said breathlessly. I had to be.
Tristian’s helmet met mine. “Good.”
I wondered what he hid behind his helmet. Did Tristian smile, or did he hear the fear in my voice?

A hissing began behind us, large metal doors slowly peeling apart. I was going above. The realization slammed into me as the light started to peek through the growing crack. The temperature reading began to drop as cold air slithered in. Fresh air. The metal doors ground open.
We’re almost there, Lara; hang on. We’re almost there.
Lara’s pale, too thin form heavy in my hands, slowing me down as I attempted to sprint. The entrance coming into view.
It’s okay, Sash. Go on without me.
No, I will not leave you. You have to hang on. They’ll help you. They’ll save you.
It’s okay. I’ll be with Mom, Dad, and Eli. I’m ready. I’m not scared. I’m ready.
No, no. You have to stay with me.
Her eyes closed.
Lara, don’t leave me. Stay with me. I’m not ready. I’m not ready. Help me.
Blinding lights forced my eyes closed, so all I saw was her pale face. She wouldn’t grow to be beautiful.
“As long as there’s life,” Tristian called out to the group.
“We defend it with our own,” Unit Seven answered.
I opened my eyes. There was still life. Not my family, but there was life. I couldn’t fail again.
“We go out as one,” Tristian continued. Time froze as the bright light framed his massive form. His back to the world above. His focus on his unit.
A chill ran up my spine as Unit Seven answered:
“We come back as one.”

Excerpted from Beneath by Ariel Sullivan. Copyright © 2026 by Ariel Sullivan. All rights reserved. No part of this excerpt may be reproduced or reprinted without permission in writing from the publisher.

title: ‘Chuck Norris Facts’ Creator Explains How a Ben Stiller Movie Led to the Classic Meme
summary: Chuck Norris has died at 86. Before then, he was a defining part of the internet. Creator Ian Spector tells TODAY.com about how Chuck Norris Facts came to be.
story: There was probably a time when it sounded strange: Chuck Norris, Internet legend.
Yes, the martial arts icon and action star of the small and big screen. That Chuck Norris.

The “Walker, Texas Ranger” star, whose family announced March 20 he died at the age of 86, would grow to become as much a part of the Internet era as waiting in a queue for Taylor Swift concert tickets, thanks to memes centered around the actor that Ian Spector started more than two decades ago.
“I was just a teenager who had a website on a random little domain name,” Spector tells TODAY.com about his own website.

The memes, of course, are those “Chuck Norris facts” you’ve probably seen that amplify how tough Norris was — something like, “In an emergency, Chuck Norris doesn’t call 911 ... 911 calls Chuck Norris.” Or, “Chuck Norris doesn't wear a watch. He decides what time it is.” Or, “A bulletproof vest wears Chuck Norris for protection.” You get the idea.

The “facts” originated in 2005 after Spector noticed a forum on a website called Something Awful that presented fake facts about Vin Diesel, he recalled to his alma mater's newspaper in 2006. Spector wanted to create a site that gathered all of these “facts,” and allow people to submit their own. He created a “fact generator” and shared the link on Something Awful.
“I never expected any of this to ever take off, right? Like I posted a link in a forum once and the next morning, there were 10,000 hits, and (it) grew from there,” Spector says.

Spector then created a poll seeking to find a new celebrity for people to target with their “facts.” Norris was the clear winner. To put that in terms of the subject at hand, the response proved that the Chuck Norris didn’t need the Internet — the Internet needed Chuck Norris.
Slightly more than 20 years ago, the idea of a meme — a type of iterative image, text, video or concept that could spread organically on the internet — took off.
Spector believes Norris himself set the stage for the meme’s popularity, thanks to his appearance in the 2004 Ben Stiller movie, “Dodgeball.”

“I think that also was a kind of catalyst, because there were people who knew and loved his work from the ‘70s and ‘80s, and ‘Walker, Texas Ranger’ in the ‘90s, and there’s this brand new audience of people who kind of just knew him as the guy that saved the day for the winning team in ‘Dodgeball,’ and that kind of grew into its own ecology,” he says.
“I chalk it up to he also is just incredibly consistent in terms of his values and where he stands. Like, you know what you’re getting with Chuck Norris.”
Spector would go on to write five books of facts about Norris. The first one, “The Truth About Chuck Norris: 400 Facts About the World’s Greatest Human,” was a New York Times bestseller.
It was a full-blown phenomenon, turning Norris into a quasi-Paul Bunyan larger than life character. It all begs the question: Did the meme concept itself originate with Norris?
“The short answer is, yeah, and my ego likes to think so,” says Spector, while adding that different types of online humor may have existed “before my time.”
Spector also points out that “people weren’t just writing text over images quite yet” when Chuck Norris facts exploded onto the scene.
“But the meme as a form of literacy, or the form like ‘here’s the template for a joke’ that we all get and everybody gets to participate in’ sprouted from the facts, he says.
Spector says Norris “holds a special place in Internet culture.”
“I’m glad that the world was able to find all the amazing greatness inside of Chuck Norris, something that could brighten somebody’s day,” he says.
“I’m glad that the world was able to find all the amazing greatness inside of Chuck Norris, something that could brighten somebody’s day."
Norris facts live on. There is a Chuck Norris facts website that Spector is not affiliated with that exists today, which speaks to its enduring relevance.
“I think it worked, because he was that guy, like he had that toughness and disciplining career. I kind of just built the joke system around that,” he says.

Spector, who says he and Norris had a “nice and surreal meeting” in 2006, credits the star and his wife, Gena O’Kelley, for “being incredible for running with a joke.” Indeed, Norris embraced the mania, reading some of the facts on TV and writing his own book of factts.

Norris’ legacy was already written when a new period about his life as an Internet star would be carved, in large part, by Spector. And perhaps no other further proof is needed than in the outpouring of inquiries Spector received following Norris’ death.
“Yeah, there’s been quite a lot of interest from around the world,” he says.

title: What Happened with Chappell Roan in Brazil? Singer Responds to Allegations Her Security Made a Child Cry
summary: Chappell Roan has denied that a member of her security team scolded the 11-year-old stepdaughter of soccer star Jorginho Frello.
story: Chappell Roan has denied that a member of her security team scolded an 11-year-old girl and her mother at a hotel after the girl's stepfather, an Italian-Brazilian soccer star, wrote a message chastising Roan and accusing a security guard of reducing the girl to tears.

The pop star responded in a video to her Instagram story on March 22, one day after soccer player Jorginho Frello posted on his Instagram that a security guard had a "disproportionate" response to his stepdaughter walking by Roan's table at a hotel.
People ranging from Frello's wife to the mayor of Rio de Janeiro has since weighed in on the story. Here's what to know about the alleged controversy.
Who Are Jorginho Frello, Catherine Harding and Chappell Roan?
Roan, 28, won the Best New Artist Grammy in 2025 and is known for hits like "Hot to Go!" and "Pink Pony Club."
At the time of the alleged incident, Roan was in São Paulo to perform at the popular Lollapalooza Brazil music festival.

Frello is known as Jorginho, a soccer star who has played for the Italian national team and currently plays midfielder for Clube de Regatas do Flamengo, a professional team that is part of Brazil's highest-level soccer league. He previously played for two of the best-known teams in the world, Chelsea and Arsenal, in the English Premier League.
Harding is an Irish singer who is Frello's wife and the mother of the 11-year-old girl. She was with her during the time of the alleged incident.
Harding shares daughter Ada with British actor Jude Law, a father of seven who was not present at the hotel during the alleged encounter.
Why Did Jorginho Call Out Chappell Roan?

Frello posted a lengthy message on his Instagram story on March 21 — which was re-shared on X by Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Cavaliere — accusing a member of Roan's security team of berating his wife and stepdaughter and leaving the girl in tears.
Frello said that Harding and Ada were coincidentally staying at the same hotel as Roan and had planned on attending her show in São Paulo that night because his stepdaughter is a big fan.
He said that the girl thought she spotted Roan while she was eating breakfast with Harding, so she went to see if it was her.
"She simply walked past the singer's table, looked to confirm it was her, smiled, and went back to sit with her mum," Frello wrote. "She didn't say anything, didn't ask for anything."
Frello then said a large security guard approached Harding and their daughter and had a response that was "completely disproportionate."

He claims the security guard told his wife that she shouldn't allow her daughter to "disrespect" or "harass" other people. Frello said the security guard threatened to file a complaint with the hotel as his stepdaughter sat there "in tears."
Frello said Ada was "completely shaken" and "cried a lot." He then directed a message to Roan.
"WITHOUT YOUR FANS, YOU WOULD BE NOTHING," he wrote. "AND TO THE FANS, SHE DOES NOT DESERVE YOUR AFFECTION."
How Did Chappell Roan Respond?
Roan shared a video on her Instagram story of her lying in bed and reacting to Frello's message.

"I'm just going to tell my half of the story of what happened today with a mother and child who were involved with a security guard who is not my personal security," she said. "I didn't even see a woman and a child. No one came up to me. No one bothered me. I was just sitting at breakfast in my hotel."
"I did not ask the security guard to go up and talk to this mother and child," she continued. "They did not come up to me. They weren't doing anything."
Roan has previously called out how some fans have treated her. In 2024, she wrote an Instagram post about "harassment" and "predatory behavior" she said she has experienced from superfans.
However, she clarified that stance in her video message in response to Frello's post.

"I do not hate people who are fans of my music," she said. "I do not hate children. That is crazy. I'm sorry to the mother and child that someone was assuming something that you would do something, that if you felt uncomfortable, that makes me really sad. You did not deserve that."
What Did Catherine Harding Say?
Harding shared her side of the encounter in an Instagram video on March 22, saying that she was “shocked” at what happened.

“I know that Chappell has responded saying that it wasn’t her security, and she didn’t do it,” Harding said. “So, 100% this security guard was not a security guard of the hotel, that’s what I can say. He looks after artists. So I don’t know if it was her personal security guard, but he was with her, so that is all I know.”
Harding said she and Ada came to São Paulo specifically to see Roan’s show because her daughter wanted tickets as a birthday present.
She reiterated her husband’s version of events that her daughter simply walked by Roan’s table to see if it was her but did not have her phone, did not take any photos and did not approach Roan.

A “very large” and “intimidating” security guard then approached their table and scolded her by saying she needed to teach her daughter to respect other’s privacy, according to Harding. She said she tried to explain to the man that her daughter is around celebrities frequently and “would never cross someone’s boundaries.”

“I was shocked,” Harding said. “I said to (the security guard) at the time, if it’s the case that she doesn’t want people to even look at her, she is very welcome to eat her breakfast in her room because this here is a public area, and I am a paying guest as well as her, so if I want to walk past someone’s table and look at them, I have every right to do so. I am not approaching her, I am not doing anything to make her feel intimidated or nothing.”
Harding added that she and her daughter ended up not going to Roan’s show.
“I hope that maybe if it wasn’t her that she learns not to allow people that work for her to treat people like this,” Harding said.
Why Did the Mayor of Rio Weigh In?
In the wake of Frello's initial Instagram post, the mayor of Rio de Janeiro wrote on X in Portuguese that he will ban Roan from playing one of Rio's biggest music festivals.

"I mean that as long as I’m in charge of our city — this young lady @ChappellRoan will never perform at Todo Mundo no Rio!" Eduardo Cavaliere wrote, according to the English translation on X. "I doubt that Shakira @shakira @Shakira_Brasil would do that! By the way, @FrelloJorginho your little one is already the guest of honor from the organization in May!"
Frello responded to the post with a pair of heart emojis.

Todo Mundo no Rio is a set of massive free concerts in May that are held on Copacabana Beach. Colombian superstar Shakira is headlining this year's events, and Madonna and Lady Gaga have performed at previous editions.

title: All About Sheinelle Jones’ Book Tour and How to Preorder ‘Through Mom’s Eyes’
summary: Learn about Sheinelle Jones' debut book "Through Mom's Eyes," how to preorder and a list of Sheinelle's book tour dates.
story: Sheinelle Jones is gearing up for the release of her debut book and her first-ever book tour this spring.
“Through Mom’s Eyes,” out officially on April 14, is a collection of life lessons shared by women who raised extraordinary people and encouraged by her late husband, Uche Ojeh.
“‘Through Mom’s Eyes’ celebrates the quiet, everyday love that shapes who we become,” her publisher, Putnam Books, said in a press release.

TODAY viewers may recognize the title of “Through Mom’s Eyes” as it was the name of Sheinelle’s very own TODAY series, in which she interviewed moms of celebrities and public figures. In each installment, she picked their brains on their parenting styles and tips and even used their advice to help raise her three children. She shares her kids with her late husband of 17 years, Uche Ojeh, who passed away last year after a battle with brain cancer.
The book “Through Mom’s Eyes” features the insights Sheinelle has learned from dozens of women, mixed with stories from Sheinelle’s journey of finding light through her grief.
Putnam also announced that Sheinelle will celebrate her book’s arrival with a tour that includes special guests, like Al Roker.
See the list of stops and more details below.

title: Lea Michele Reveals the Special Mementos She Keeps in Her Dressing Room at 'Chess' Theater
summary: Lea Michele has kept a few very special mementos backstage with her while while starring in the hit Broadway revival of the 1986 musical "Chess."On the March 22 episode of Sunday Sitdown, the former "Glee" star took TODAY's Willie Geist on a tour of the musical's home, the Imperial Theatre, the same venue where Michele made her Broadway debut as an 8-year-old actor in the original Broadway run of "Les Misérables.""I found a picture of me from 1995 when I was in 'Les Mis,' and it was me and righ
story: Lea Michele has kept a few very special mementos backstage with her while while starring in the hit Broadway revival of the 1986 musical "Chess."

On the March 22 episode of Sunday Sitdown, the former "Glee" star took TODAY's Willie Geist on a tour of the musical's home, the Imperial Theatre, the same venue where Michele made her Broadway debut as an 8-year-old actor in the original Broadway run of "Les Misérables."

"I found a picture of me from 1995 when I was in 'Les Mis,' and it was me and right behind me was a 'Chess' playbill," Michele told Willie. "And then the first time I walked into this theater with 'Chess,' I looked right here, and here is where I took that picture."

The 39-year-old entertainer, who dazzled audiences after taking over the lead role in the 2022 revival of "Funny Girl," has kept the throwback snapshot of her in "Les Misérables" in her dressing room since "Chess" debuted in November 2025.
“I remember saying to my parents after my first performance (in 'Les Mis'), ‘I want to do this for the rest of my life. Don’t ever tell me to stop. Don’t ever let me stop,’” she said.
The photo serves as a reminder to Michele, who grew up in the Bronx and New Jersey the daughter of a nurse and a deli owner, of how she made her wildest dreams come true.

"I look at this picture every day before I go out there, and I’m like, I think she would be so happy, and she would be so grateful that I listened to her when she said don’t ever let me stop doing this," she said.
Also in Michele's dressing room: drawings her own little ones, son Ever, 5, and daughter Emery, 1, made for her.

"I brought my children here the other day. My son he’s a little, like second show, you know? He was around for 'Funny Girl,'" she told Willie. "And my daughter was just running around here with this confidence that I was like, 'OK, I see you.'"
"But I looked at my mom, and my mom was like, 'I can’t believe I’m here with your children and I used to walk you through that door,'" she said.
"You know, my son is gonna be 6, so it’s only two years younger than I was. So it’s a real incredible moment for my family," she added.

In "Chess," Michele plays Florence Vassy, a brilliant Cold War-era chess strategist who becomes entangled in a love triangle with an American chess master and his Russian rival, played, respectively, by Aaron Tveit and Nicholas Christopher.
The musical features music by ABBA members Benny Andersson and Björn Ulvaeus, lyrics by Tim Rice of “Evita” fame and a new book by Danny Strong ("Empire").
Michele describes the role of the passionate Florence as her first "woman" character.

"This is the first real adult that I’ve ever played before," she told Geist. "I can’t wait to get on this stage, and be Florence, and to feel that strength and that confidence, and she’s living in this world of men, and what does that mean? And I just love it."

title: How to Watch 'Saturday Night Live UK’ in the U.S.
summary: The British version of "Saturday Night Live" will be available to stream on Peacock, beginning March 22.
story: Live from the other side of the pond … it’s Saturday night.
The new U.K. version of "Saturday Night Live," premieringi n March, will follow the blueprint of its American counterpart, with guest hosts and live musical guests.
In fact, “SNL” legend Tina Fey will host the premiere episode, and even starred in a cheeky promo with the cast, in which she did a Mary Poppins impression while plugging her appearance.

“‘Saturday Night Live’ is one of the most influential comedy brands in the world and bringing the UK edition to Peacock is a natural extension of that legacy,” NBCUniversal Entertainment chief business officer Liz Jenkins said in a statement about the show.
“As the streaming home of ‘SNL,’ Peacock is proud to introduce bold new British voices to a global stage.”

“Saturday Night Live UK” will kick off its first season with cast members Hammed Animashaun, Ayoade Bamgboye, Larry Dean, Celeste Dring, George Fouracres, Ania Magliano, Annabel Marlow, Al Nash, Jack Shep, Emma Sidi and Paddy Young.
Riz Ahmed, who was nominated for a best actor Academy Award for his work in “Sound of Metal,” will host one of the episodes. He has no idea what to expect.
“I was hoping you could tell me,” he told Dylan Dreyer, Craig Melvin and Kaylee Hartung March 17 on the 3rd hour of TODAY.

“Cause ‘SNL’ is new to the UK, right? It's such an amazing institution here. It has such a kind of lore and I think it'll be really fascinating to see how we can kind of honor that, but also kind of do our own thing with it. British humor is very specific. And if we do our own thing with it, don’t be mad.”
He then pointed to a British series that made its way to America and did very well.
“You guys stole ‘The Office' from us, so we’re going to take ‘SNL’ from you,” he said.

While “Saturday Night Live UK” gears up to make its own mark, the original “Saturday Night Live” continues to chug along, after its milestone 50th season wrapped last year. Created by Lorne Michaels and serving as a launching pad for scores of comedians, from Chevy Chase and Bill Murray to Tina Fey and Bowen Yang, it’s become a pop culture staple, currently in the midst of its 51st season. And sometimes the show is so funny, even the hosts (we're looking at you, Ryan Gosling) have trouble holding it together.
“SNL” has received 338 Emmy nominations during its run and won 93 Emmy Awards, according to the Television Academy.
What Time Does 'Saturday Night Live UK' Air in the U.S.?
“Saturday Night Live UK” will premiere March 21 on SKY One and stream on NOW TV in Britain.
The episode airs at 10 p.m. GMT, which is 2 p.m. ET.
How to Watch ‘Saturday Night Live UK’ in the U.S.
Peacock will carry episodes of “Saturday Night Live UK,” the British version of the iconic late-night series, the day after each episode originally airs.
New episodes will be available to stream on Peacock starting March 22.

title: 'Buffy the Vampire Slayer' Star Nicholas Brendon Dies at 54
summary: Nicholas Brendon, known for his role as Xander Harris on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” has died
story: Nicholas Brendon, known for his role as Xander Harris on “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” has died.
His family confirmed his death to TODAY.com, as well as shared the news on Brendon's social media on March 20, expressing their heartache and love for the actor.

“We are heartbroken to share the passing of our brother and son, Nicholas Brendon. He passed in his sleep of natural causes,” the statement begins. “Most people know Nicky for his work as an actor and for the characters he brought to life over the years. In recent years Nicky has found his passion in painting and art.”
The actor’s family states that Brendon “loved to share his enthusiastic talent with his family, friends and fans.”

“He was passionate, sensitive, and endlessly driven to create. Those who truly knew him understood that his art was one of the purest reflections of who he was,” the statement continues, before the family asks for privacy during this time “as we grieve his loss and celebrate the life of a man who lived with intensity, imagination, and heart.”
“Thank you to everyone who has shown love and support,” it concludes.
The statement also included a number of photos of Brendon with his family, including twin brother Kelly Donovan, as well as his photography and art.
Brendon appeared on “Buffy,” starring Sarah Michelle Gellar and Alyson Hannigan, among others, from 1997 to 2003.
Hannigan posted a tribute to “sweet Nicky” following news of his death.

“My Sweet Nicky, thank you for years of laughter, love and Dodgers. I will think of you every time I see a rocking chair.  I love you. RIP,” she wrote on Instagram, next to a photo of the two from “Buffy.”
Emma Caulfield, who portrayed Xander’s love interest on “Buffy” also shared a message on her Instagram story.
“No word yet. I love you Nicky,” she wrote over a video of the actor. “Let this clip of us giving it our best be a placeholder. Rest Nicky, Rest.”
She signed it with a broken heart and her former character’s name, “Anya.”
In 2023, he celebrated the 20th anniversary of the hit show's finale, sharing a cast photo from the final episode and reflecting on its legacy.

“It’s hard to believe Buffy The Vampire Slayer signed off 20 years ago today (in the US),” he wrote in part on Instagram. “I still meet people nearly every day that were fans of the show and it can sometimes feel like we just finished filming and not that two decades have gone by.”
He added that he loved that so many people connected with the show and characters.

“And despite some of the bittersweet memories, I’ll always be so grateful I had the opportunity to play Xander and that it gave me the chance to meet so many of you,” he added. “Thank you for holding a place in your heart for the show for all of these years. I’m just as big a fan as all of you and I miss watching it as much as I miss filming it.”

Brendon’s additional credits included the movies “Celeste in the City, “Relative Chaos,” “Redwood,” “Attack of the Morningside Monster” and “Coherence.” He also appeared on shows like “Faking It,” “Criminal Minds,” “Private Practice” and “Hollywood Heights.”

The actor did face a number of legal troubles over the years amid struggles with drugs and alcohol. He faced multiple arrests including a 2014 incident after a drunken dispute at an Idaho hotel. He was also arrested in 2021 and was charged with felony prescription fraud. A plea agreement was later reached, WTHI reported in 2022.
He spoke about his struggles with substance abuse and depression during a Dr. Phil episode. In 2022, People reported that Brendon was hospitalized for a cardiac issue.

title: When and What Time Does ‘BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang’ Stream on Netflix
summary: BTS is officially back and set to take the stage for the first time in years. Find out how to watch.
story: BTS is officially back and set to take the stage for the first time in years.
The superstar group are celebrating the release of their new album, “Arirang,” out now, with a special live concert from the historic Gwanghwamun in Seoul.

Airing on Netflix, “BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang” will have BTS members RM, Jin, Suga, J-Hope, Jimin, V and Jung Kook make their highly anticipated return after they took a break to complete their military services.
BTS fans, known as Army, have been anxiously waiting to see the seven singers perform live — and in just a few hours they will get to.
However, a day before the reunion concert, BigHit Music announced that RM suffered an ankle injury “and was taken to a hospital to undergo a detailed examination and treatment.”
The singer is expected to be on stage but his choreography “will be partially limited,” the statement reads.

The concert is just the start of the group’s comeback. The promotion for their album includes an appearance in New York on March 23 for a special Spotify event. They will also be on “The Tonight Show Starring Jimmy Fallon” on March 25 and 26.
Additionally, Netflix will drop their documentary, “BTS: The Return,” on March 27. And that's not all: The group will kick off their “BTS World Tour Arirang” on April 9 in South Korea.
But first, here is everything you need to know about the BTS concert, “BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang.”
When Is ‘BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang’ Happening?
“BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang” will take place on Saturday, March 21.
Where to Watch ‘BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang’
The reunion concert will stream on Netflix.
What Time Will ‘BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang’ Stream?
The concert will stream live around the world, and the time depends on where you are located.
The BTS concert begins at 8 p.m. Korean Standard Time, 7 a.m. ET and 4 a.m. PT.
For those unable to watch it live, the BTS concert will stream on Netflix after.
What to Expect During ‘BTS The Comeback Live | Arirang’

BTS is expected to perform songs from their new album, “Arirang,” which was released March 20. The group will hopefully also sing some of their major hits, like “Dynamite,” “Permission to Dance,” “Butter,” “Mic Drop” and many more.

title: Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen's Relationship History Explained
summary: Who are Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen? What is their relationship history? What are the allegations against them?
story: “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” star Taylor Frankie Paul’s relationship with ex-boyfriend Dakota Mortensen has come under scrutiny in the lead-up to the premiere of her season of “The Bachelorette.”
The couple was at the center of an “ongoing investigation,” three sources close to the matter told NBC News on March 17, days before “The Bachelorette” was meant to premiere on March 22.

Draper City Police Department in Utah, the state where Paul and Mortensen both live, told People on March 16 that the reality stars were connected to an open “domestic assault investigation.” The department declined to confirm the report to NBC News, stating, “It is the practice of the department not to release details related to active investigations.”

Then, on March 19, TMZ published a video of Paul attacking and throwing chairs at Mortensen in front of her daughter that Mortensen reportedly filmed in 2023. NBC News has not independently verified the circumstances and authenticity of the video. The footage was allegedly related to an arrest and misdemeanor charges Paul faced in 2023. Paul pled guilty in abeyance to aggravated assault in August 2023, court records show. The other charges against her were dropped.

Later that day, her season of “The Bachelorette” was canceled. “In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” a Disney spokesperson said in a statement given to TODAY.com. “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” filming has also been paused.
Paul also released a statement.

“It’s sad to see the latest installment of his never-ending, desperate, attention-seeking, destructive campaign to harm Taylor without any regard for the consequences for their child,” the statement said, speaking of Mortensen. “Releasing an old video, which conveniently omits context, on their son’s birthday is a reprehensible attempt to distract from his own behavior. Thankfully, the public has seen this act before and knows who he is and sadly, many will recognize this pattern of manipulation, both in his actions on the show, and from their own experiences.”
TODAY.com has reached out to Mortensen for comment and has not heard back by the time of publication.
What is the couple’s history? We’re sharing everything you need to know about their complicated relationship and where it stands today.
Who Are Taylor Frankie Paul and Dakota Mortensen?
Paul is credited as the creator of #MomTok, the loose coalition of Mormon influencers who have built up independent followings on TikTok and other social media platforms, but also collaborate.

She also was responsible for its implosion in 2022, when she released a TikTok Live saying she and her ex-husband, Tate Paul had been involved in a “soft swinging” group. Many of the show’s other cast members have denied participating, although Miranda McWhorter said she participated.
Paul said that she “messed up” while participating in soft swinging and broke the boundaries she and her husband had set.
In 2022, Paul introduced her followers to Mortensen. Their son was born in 2024.
In addition to the son she shares with Mortensen, Paul also has two children with her ex-husband, Tate Paul.
Where Does Paul and Mortensen’s Relationship Stand As Of March 2026?
Paul and Mortensen are currently part of an “ongoing investigation,” according to three sources close to the matter who spoke with NBC News on March 17 on the condition of anonymity.

On March 16, the Draper City Police Department told People that Paul and Mortensen are connected to an open “domestic assault investigation.” When contacted by NBC News, the department declined to confirm the report, stating, “It is the practice of the department not to release details related to active investigations.”
While speaking with People, the spokesperson for the Draper City Police Department said “allegations have been made in both directions” and “contact was made with involved parties” on Feb. 24 and 25.

On March 18, the West Jordan Police Department in Utah told NBC News that it received a domestic violence allegation involving Paul and Mortensen. The department said it did not know the date or time of the reported incident and declined to give additional details.

On March 19, TMZ posted a video that shows Paul attacking and throwing chairs at Mortensen in front of her daughter. The video was reportedly filmed in 2023. NBC News has not independently verified the circumstances and authenticity of the video.

On March 19, Mortensen filed a petition for a protective order against Paul, per documents obtained by NBC News. The documents, which were filed in Utah’s Salt Lake County, were sent back for a correction and an amended petition.
On the same day, Mortensen shared the following statement with People.

“As anyone who has seen the video will understand, this is a deeply upsetting situation,” the statement reads. “I am, unfortunately, used to these baseless claims about me and our relationship, which I categorically deny. I am focusing on our son and his safety, and hope that Taylor will do the same.”
TODAY.com has reached out to Mortensen for comment.
In a phone interview with NBC News, Paul addressed the release of the video, saying, “I will have my truth.”
What to Know About Paul and Mortensen’s Relationship History

“The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” has given viewers a look into the turmoil in Paul and Mortensen’s relationship. Speaking to the camera, Paul’s family and friends express the worry that she and her boyfriend communicated in a volatile manner.

Paul was arrested in February 2023 on charges including assault and committing domestic violence when a child was present. She reached a plea deal in her assault case in late August 2023. She pled guilty to aggravated assault, and the other charges against her were dismissed with prejudice, meaning they were permanently dismissed, according to court documents viewed by The Salt Lake Tribune.
In an interview with TODAY.com, the TikToker reflected on a moment of turmoil in her relationship, though didn’t explicitly refer to the arrest.

“I forgot the cameras were there and Dakota and I got into a fight ... My true emotions come out and I think I got a little angry and open up. I think that’s the first time I share what’s going on in my head. Not that I regret doing that. It opened up to be honest about what was going on. Maybe I regret how I said it,” she said.
Paul was hesitant to get engaged to Mortensen while she was pregnant, citing the "lies” he told her at the beginning of their relationship as one of her main reasons.

In a confessional interview, she recalled a time at the beginning of their relationship when they weren’t exclusively dating. Paul said Mortensen lied about hanging out with his male friends but later said that he hooked up with another woman that night.
“I’ve been honest that I’m not ready to progress because I’m scared something else is going to come out. But do you blame me?” she asked.
Mortensen said that Paul regularly “self-sabotaged” their relationship.

Season 1 of ‘The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives’ ended on a cliffhanger when Paul’s friend, Mayci Neeley, called a woman who claimed to have “accidentally slept with” Mortensen before she knew that he was dating Paul.
Paul and Mortensen welcomed a child in March 2024.
The couple had previously experienced an ectopic pregnancy, which occurs when the fertilized egg implants outside the uterus and the pregnancy is not viable, per the Mayo Clinic.
Paul spoke with TODAY.com following the premiere of Season 1 in 2024. She revealed that she and Mortensen were together at the time.
“We’re in couples therapy and coaching, so they’re kind of helping us to communicate and learn all these things while taking on the loud noise, which is really cool,” she said at the time.
Paul also said her relationship with Mortensen had changed significantly since filming Season 1 of “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives.”

“Things have gotten a lot better. We’re obviously far from the end goal, because we come in (to the relationship) with such baggage, and now we’re doing TV and a lot comes with that,” the influencer said.

Flash forward to Season 2, which premiered in September 2025, and Paul struggled to trust Mortensen after she learned that he had been involved with another woman at the beginning of their relationship.
Mortensen admitted to lying in a confessional interview, saying, “I was always doing it selfishly to protect myself. I just wish I was honest with her but it’s too late.”
According to Paul, he “swore that nothing really had happened besides kissing.” Then he came forward and said “there was more to the story.”
In Season 2, Episode 1, Neeley and Paul met with the other woman, whose first name is Jenna, to learn more.
Jenna said Mortensen was mostly the one initiating their dates, which he had previously denied to Paul.

Jenna said she questioned Mortensen about his relationship with Paul when she saw a TikTok video of them. He responded that he was just talking to Paul “for clout” and to help him build his social media profile.
In a confessional interview, Mortensen said he spoke with Paul about his relationship with Jenna. Mortensen said he deleted some messages from Jenna out of fear that he would lose Paul.
“I did lie. I was always doing it selfishly to protect myself. I just wish I was honest with her but it’s too late,” he said.
Before the Season 2 premiere, Paul spoke with People about the potential of getting back together with Mortensen one day.

“I feel like right now we are individually working on ourselves and trying to heal, for not only ourselves, but for our son. So that’s been the main focus the last few months, last half of a year,” she said.
Paul wasn’t in a rush to jump back into a romance with her ex but she was keeping the door open.
“But to me, I don’t know, I never say never, but right now it’s just individually trying to heal and work on things we needed to work on,” she said.
During the Season 2 reunion special, host Nick Viall asked Paul where her relationship with Mortensen stood at that time.
“We are just individually needing to do so much self work on ourselves to be better parents for our son. I mean, our friends all can see that I do get very giddy around him,” she responded.
“I did want Dakota. And from day one, did I show it in the best way? No. Was I fully open? No, cause I always had an intuition something was off. And granted, I was very correct on that,” she added.
Mortensen said he “screwed up” when he wasn’t honest with Paul early on in their relationship about his relationships with other women.
Paul took responsibility for her own behavior and said, “I was awful and I was very reactive and mean because I think when you’re lied to, I just became so angry.”
Next, Mortensen expressed his love for Paul and said he believes he’s capable of being a faithful partner. When asked if Paul agreed, she said, “Maybe one day. If I were to answer today, no.”
In September 2025, ABC announced that Paul was selected to be the leading lady of Season 22 of “The Bachelorette.”
While appearing on the “Call Her Daddy” podcast, the reality star addressed what this meant for her on-again-off-again relationship with Mortensen.
“I’m assuming going on ‘The Bachelorette’ means that you two are done for good,” host Alex Cooper said.
“Yes. It’s over and we’ll see that play out (on ‘The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives’),” Paul replied, adding that she made the final call to end the relationship.
“So, you officially think it’s over with him?” Cooper asked.
“I don’t think. I know,” Paul replied.
The reality star also said that she had not “confirmed” her appointment as the new “Bachelorette” star with her ex.
“He’ll know Wednesday,” she said, referring to the release date of the podcast she was filming.
When asked how she thought Mortensen would react to the news, Paul said, “Sad.”

When “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” Season 3 debuted in November 2025, viewers witnessed Paul and Mortensen navigate a new layer of betrayal in their relationship as Paul learned that Mortensen had previously exchanged flirty messages with a close friend of her family.
The drama spilled over into Season 4, which premiered in March 2026.

title: ‘Secret Lives of Mormon Wives’ Stars Speak Out Amid Taylor Frankie Paul’s Controversy
summary: “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” cast members Layla Taylor and Miranda McWhorter are weighing in about the controversy involving co-star Taylor Frankie Paul.
story: “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” cast members Layla Taylor and Miranda McWhorter are weighing in about the controversy involving co-star Taylor Frankie Paul.

On March 19, TMZ shared a video from 2023 that showed Paul attacking and throwing chairs at her ex, Dakota Mortensen, while her daughter was in the room. It appears Mortensen was filming the incident. Paul is a mother of three, and the former couple share a 2-year-old son.
Taylor and McWhorter addressed the altercation in statements shared on their Instagram story March 20.
Taylor wrote, “My personal history as a survivor makes it impossible for me to stay silent. I stand firmly against domestic violence in any form.”
She added she was “keeping (Paul’s) children in my thoughts, hoping for their safety above all else.”

McWhorter also condemned domestic violence in her statement, writing, “I’ve honestly been a little at a loss for words trying to process this all. Everything has been really heavy to see. Domestic violence and abuse of any kind is something I take very seriously.”
“As a human, and especially a mother, I cannot support that kind of behavior,” she continued. “My heart is with the children in this and I hope they’re safe, supported, and surrounded by love.”
Prior to the latest headlines, members of the cast expressed concerns about working with Paul in a Zoom meeting with ABC executives earlier this month,  NBC News reported in a digital exclusive.

NBC News obtained audio of members voicing their issues, with one saying, “It’s a dangerous situation, it’s a sad situation, and we don’t know how to navigate it, because Taylor is our friend.” One also said they did not feel comfortable filming while Paul was being investigated.
NBC News reported earlier this week that filming for “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” was paused due to an investigation involving Paul and Mortensen.

That investigation was pertaining to a “domestic assault investigation,” People reported. The Draper City Police Department told the outlet that “allegations have been made in both directions” and “contact was made with involved parties” on Feb. 24 and 25.
The department told NBC News that it could not confirm the information because “it is the practice of the department not to release details related to active investigations.”
Following the video release of the 2023 altercation, Disney announced that it will no longer air Paul’s season of “The Bachelorette,” which was scheduled to premiere March 22 on ABC.

“In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” a Disney spokesperson confirmed in a statement sent to TODAY.com.

Paul issued a statement via a spokesperson to NBC News about the cancellation and controversy, saying that she is “very grateful for ABC’s support as she prioritizes her family’s safety and security.”

“After years of silently suffering extensive mental and physical abuse as well as threats of retaliation, Taylor is finally gaining the strength to face her accuser and taking steps to ensure that she and her children are protected from any further harm,” the statement said. “There are too many women who are suffering in silence as they survive aggressive, jealous ex-partners who refuse to let them move on with their lives. Taylor has remained silent out of fear of further abuse, retaliation, and public shaming. She is currently exploring all of her options, seeking support, and preparing to own and share her story.”

Her representative also told NBC News in a statement about the video, “It’s sad to see the latest installment of his never-ending, desperate, attention-seeking, destructive campaign to harm Taylor without any regard for the consequences for their child.”
As for Mortensen, he commented on the video in a statement to People.

“As anyone who has seen the video will understand, this is a deeply upsetting situation,” he said. “I am, unfortunately, used to these baseless claims about me and our relationship, which I categorically deny. I am focusing on our son and his safety, and hope that Taylor will do the same.”
He has since been granted a temporary protective order, NBC News confirmed. A hearing has been scheduled for April 7.

title: ‘Secret Lives of Mormon Wives’ Cast Raised Concerns About Taylor Frankie Paul in Video Meeting with ABC
summary: Taylor Frankie Paul’s fellow “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” cast members raised concerns about continuing to work with her during a Zoom meeting with ABC executives earlier this month, NBC News reported in a digital exclusive
story: Taylor Frankie Paul’s fellow “Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” cast members raised concerns about continuing to work with her during a Zoom meeting with ABC executives earlier this month, NBC News reported in a digital exclusive.

Members of the cast of the Hulu reality series shared their concerns about their careers and the future of the show amid an investigation involving Paul and her ex, Dakota Mortensen, during the roughly 30-minute meeting on March 7, according to audio obtained by NBC News.
A spokesperson for the Draper Police Department in Utah told People earlier this week that Paul and Mortensen were involved in an open “domestic assault investigation” from February.

One cast member told the executives during the virtual meeting that they did not feel comfortable filming during the investigation into Paul, who is a mother of three children, including a 2-year-old son she shares with Mortensen.
“It’s a dangerous situation, it’s a sad situation, and we don’t know how to navigate it, because Taylor is our friend,” the cast member said.
The NBC News report comes after Disney, the parent company of ABC, announced March 19 the network was scrapping Paul’s upcoming season of “The Bachelorette” three days before its scheduled premiere.

Disney made its decision shortly after TMZ published a newly released video of an altercation between Paul and Mortensen in Utah in 2023. This video is reportedly connected to Paul’s arrest in 2023; she faced charges of assault, criminal mischief and domestic violence in the presence of a child, according to Salt Lake County Sheriff’s Office records obtained by NBC News.

Her arrest, which was documented on the first season of “Mormon Wives,” led to her pleading guilty in abeyance in 2025 to an aggravated assault charge. The four other charges she faced were dismissed with prejudice, according to court documents obtained by NBC News.

In the 2023 video published by TMZ, Paul appears to push and kick Mortensen as he repeatedly tells her, “Let me go.” Paul also appears to attack Mortensen with a chair as her daughter, who was in the room during the altercation, can be heard crying. It’s not clear what happened before the start of the video.
NBC News has not independently verified the TMZ video.

A source with knowledge of ABC’s Zoom call with the “Mormon Wives” cast members told NBC News the call happened so that ABC could tell them “we care about their safety, and they should not feel pressured to do press or film the show.”
The source spoke with NBC News on the condition of anonymity.

Three Disney executives attended the Zoom meeting, including Rob Mills, the executive vice president of unscripted and alternative entertainment at Walt Disney Television, according to the person who shared the audio with NBC News.
As cast members provided their accounts of Paul’s alleged behavior during the meeting, Mills said, “I don’t know a lot, nor do I want to know too much,” according to the audio.
Mills did not address the police investigation into Paul’s behavior, the source with knowledge of the Zoom call told NBC News.
“This is not me putting my head in the sand, but it’s not me — you know, I don’t want to inquire, because I don’t know what that does,” Mills said during the meeting, according to the audio.
One cast member asked Mills during the meeting if he’s “aware she’s hurt a child?” He replied, “I don’t think for us getting into it is right,” according to the audio.
Mills told the cast members that the company is “never going to hide anything that is wrong for the sake of business,” according to the audio.
Though Mills encouraged cast members to voice their concerns about Paul during filming of the show, he would not promise them the footage would not be edited.
The cast members agreed to pause filming for “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” until Paul’s legal action was resolved, according to the audio recording.

The Draper City Police Department told People on March 16 that Paul and Mortensen were part of an open “domestic assault investigation.” (The department declined to confirm the report to NBC News, stating it would not comment on active investigations.)
Disney, ABC and Hulu did not respond to NBC News’ request for comment about the police investigation regarding Paul and Mortensen.
Disney announced the cancellation of “The Bachelorette” on March 19 in a statement.

“In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” the statement read.
Paul’s rep responded to Disney’s decision in a statement to NBC News.

“Taylor is very grateful for ABC’s support as she prioritizes her family’s safety and security,” the rep said. “After years of silently suffering extensive mental and physical abuse as well as threats of retaliation, Taylor is finally gaining the strength to face her accuser and taking steps to ensure that she and her children are protected from any further harm. There are too many women who are suffering in silence as they survive aggressive, jealous ex-partners who refuse to let them move on with their lives.Taylor has remained silent out of fear of further abuse, retaliation, and public shaming. She is currently exploring all of her options, seeking support, and preparing to own and share her story.”
Mortensen responded in a March 19 statement to People.

“As anyone who has seen the video will understand, this is a deeply upsetting situation,” Mortensen said. “I am, unfortunately, used to these baseless claims about me and our relationship, which I categorically deny. I am focusing on our son and his safety, and hope that Taylor will do the same.”

title: Taylor Frankie Paul's 'Bachelorette' Contestants React to News of the Show's Cancellation
summary: The men who were on Taylor Frankie Paul's season of The Bachelorette are reacting to the show's cancellation. Here's exactly what they said.
story: Some of the men featured on Taylor Frankie Paul’s season of “The Bachelorette” are responding to the news that ABC has canceled the season just days before its March 22 premiere.

The cancellation came following the resurfacing of a video depicting Paul assaulting her ex-boyfriend, Dakota Mortensen on March 19. The same day, the network announced that Paul’s season of “The Bachelorette” would not be airing.

“In light of the newly released video just surfaced today, we have made the decision to not move forward with the new season of ‘The Bachelorette’ at this time, and our focus is on supporting the family,” a Disney spokesperson said in a statement given to TODAY.com.

The video, published by TMZ, depicts Paul attacking and throwing chairs at Mortensen in front of her daughter. The video is reportedly from 2023. Mortensen appears to be the person filming the altercation. NBC News has not independently verified the video or viewed the original video.

Paul was arrested and charged with three misdemeanors in 2023: assault, criminal mischief and domestic violence in the presence of a child, according to Salt Lake County Sheriff’s Office records obtained by NBC News.
She pleaded guilty to abeyance to aggravated assault in August 2023, per court records. The other charges against her were dropped.

Following the release of the video, Mortensen filed a petition for a protective order against Paul on March 19, according to documents obtained by NBC News. The filing was then sent back for a correction and an amended petition.
Paul’s spokesperson issued a statement March 19 given to TODAY.com.

“Taylor is very grateful for ABC’s support as she prioritizes her family’s safety and security. After years of silently suffering extensive mental and physical abuse as well as threats of retaliation, Taylor is finally gaining the strength to face her accuser and taking steps to ensure that she and her children are protected from any further harm.”

The statement continued, “There are too many women who are suffering in silence as they survive aggressive, jealous ex-partners who refuse to let them move on with their lives. Taylor has remained silent out of fear of further abuse, retaliation, and public shaming. She is currently exploring all of her options, seeking support, and preparing to own and share her story.”
NBC News has reached out to Mortensen for comment.
Now, the cast is reacting to learning their season won’t air.
How the Cast of 'The Bachelorette' Season 22 Has Responded

Matt Carroll, a 43-year-old real estate broker, took to his Instagram story to share a video from The Indianapolis Star, a news outlet in Indiana. In the video, a reporter broke down the news of the show's cancellation.
Below the video, Carroll wrote, "On pause for now."
"Thank you for everyone's support and thoughtful messages," he wrote, adding heart and rose emojis.
Malik Evans, a 30-year-old tech executive from New York, posted the following update to his Instagram story on March 19.
"I'm doing okay. Hope to share more soon. Thank you to everyone who has reached out," he wrote, also adding heart and rose emojis.
Mike Turitto, a 36-year-old brand protection manager from New Jersey, took a subtle approach.
On the morning of March 20, the Season 22 contestant posted a view from his bedroom window and captured a beautiful sunrise. He captioned the Instagram story, "It's a new day."

Conrad Ukropina, a 32-year-old startup founder from California, posted a photo of himself with Paul during filming of Season 22 in his Instagram story. He set the photo to the Miley Cyrus song, "Slide Away."
In the clip that he shared, Cyrus sings the following lyrics:
Once upon a time it was paradise
Once upon a time I was paralyzed
Think I’m gonna miss these harbour lights
But it’s time to let it go
Once upon a time it was made for us
Woke up one day, it had turned to dust
Baby, we were found, but now we’re lost
So it’s time to let it go
Clayton Johnson, a 36-year-old singer/songwriter from Tennessee,  spoke about the bonds formed in the Bachelor Mansion.

"I can honestly say this was one of the best groups of men I've ever been around. So much integrity, respect, and heart across the board. Even the tough moments never took away from that," he wrote. "Any woman would be lucky to be with any of these guys. Real integrity. Real respect. Real men."
Johnson ended his post by writing, "Love you boys," and shared a wilting rose emoji.
Doug Mason, a 28-year-old ocean lifeguard from California, shared a video message on the morning of March 20 on his Instagram story.

"Good morning everybody. In light of everything that's happened, I'm just sending out prayers to Taylor because that was her moment, and her moment was blocked. So all we can do right now is just be hopeful," he said.
Mason ended his message on a positive light, saying, "Let's stay positive and show nothing but support for people in need."
Johnnie LaRossa
Johnnie LaRossa, a baseball player from Long Island, responded to the news with an ode to the men on the show.

"Appreciate everyone reaching out. Grateful for the experience. And I've gotta say the group of guys were truly unbelievable. Nothing but respect for them. Love you boys and any woman would be lucky to have each and every one of them."

title: Chuck Norris, Martial Artist and Action Film Icon, Dies At 86
summary: "Walker, Texas Ranger" star Chuck Norris has died, his family announced March 20, less than two weeks after he turned 86.
story: Action star Chuck Norris has died, his family announced March 20. He was 86.

“It is with heavy hearts that our family shares the sudden passing of our beloved Chuck Norris yesterday morning,” his family wrote in an Instagram post accompanied by a black and white picture of the actor.

“While we would like to keep the circumstances private, please know that he was surrounded by his family and was at peace. To the world, he was a martial artist, actor, and a symbol of strength. To us, he was a devoted husband, a loving father and grandfather, an incredible brother, and the heart of our family.”
The post praised the love he tried to spread throughout his life.

“He lived his life with faith, purpose, and an unwavering commitment to the people he loved. Through his work, discipline, and kindness, he inspired millions around the world and left a lasting impact on so many lives,” the family wrote.

“While our hearts are broken, we are deeply grateful for the life he lived and for the unforgettable moments we were blessed to share with him. The love and support he received from fans around the world meant so much to him, and our family is truly thankful for it. To him, you were not just fans, you were his friends.”

The "Walker, Texas Ranger" star was recently hospitalized in Hawaii, Variety reported. In the statement, the family referenced his “recent hospitalization" without providing further details and said they “are truly grateful for the prayers and support you sent his way.”
“Thank you for loving him with us. With love, The Norris Family,” the post ended.
Norris turned 86 on March 10. He posted a video on Instagram of him boxing to commemorate the birthday.
“I don’t age. I level up,” he said in the clip and wrote in the caption. “I’m 86 today!” he continued his caption.

“Nothing like some playful action on a sunny day to make you feel young. I’m grateful for another year, good health and the chance to keep doing what I love. Thank you all for being the best fans in the world. Your support through the years has meant more to me than you’ll ever know.”

An Air Force veteran, Norris was a martial arts icon who founded the Chuck Norris System, "a Korean-based American hard style of karate,” that would also be known as Chun Kuk Do, according to the United Fighting Arts Federation.

Norris would eventually make the transition to Hollywood, with his film debut in 1968’s “The Wrecking Crew.” He would go on to star alongside Bruce Lee in the 1972 movie “The Way of the Dragon” before landing a string of action films, including “Breaker! Breaker!,” “The Delta Force,” the “Missing in Action” franchise, “Good Guys Wear Black,” “The Octagon,” “Code of Silence,” “Lone Wolf McQuade,” “Invasion U.S.A.,” “Firewalker” and “Sidekicks.”

Norris would transition to TV, playing former Marine Cordell Walker in CBS’ action series “Walker, Texas Ranger.” The show was a huge hit, running for nine seasons from 1993 until 2001. He would also return to the character in a pair of TV movies, with the last one, “Walker, Texas Ranger: Trial by Fire,” airing in 2005.
Norris did not appear on screen often in his final years, although he did appear in 2012’s “The Expendables 2.” His last credit was 2024’s “Agent Recon,” according to IMDB.
Norris’ image also became fodder for memes in the Internet age, with his face often appearing over fake facts to prove how tough he is.

“Chuck Norris kills 100% of germs,” “In an emergency, 911 calls him,” “When the Hulk gets really angry, he turns into Chuck Norris,” were among the phrases that have made the rounds on the internet over the years.

title: Cockroaches, surveillance and 16-hour days: The life of a North Korean worker in Russia
summary: Workers at Russian construction sites are among more than 100,000 North Koreans being exploited worldwide as part of a state-sponsored labor program, researchers say in a new report.
story: For North Koreans, being sent to work abroad is often seen as a precious chance to earn money for their families while getting a rare glimpse of the outside world. But workers arriving in Russia find themselves working long hours in unbearable conditions, only to owe more than they make, researchers say in a new report.

They are among more than 100,000 North Korean nationals being exploited as part of a state-sponsored labor program that operates in 40 countries, according to Global Rights Compliance, an international human rights foundation based in The Hague.

Each year the North Korean program is estimated to generate $500 million in foreign currency revenue for the reclusive, nuclear-armed state, with workers spread across construction, textiles, medicine, information technology, food service and other industries. The practice also provides an economic boost for Russia, which faces a critical labor shortage more than four years into its war with Ukraine.

“Because of the ever-growing close ties between Russia and North Korea, it is concerning that this amount of money going into the regime is funding [North Korea’s] military ambition,” Yeji Kim, North Korea adviser at Global Rights Compliance, told NBC News on Tuesday.
North Korea officially denies the existence of forced labor, though it has been widely documented by the United Nations, the U.S. State Department and others.

title: Russian drones attack downtown Lviv in broad daylight
summary: Russian forces launched a drone attack in broad daylight on the center of Lviv, a city in western Ukraine that had been spared much of the war's fighting.
story: Russian forces launched a drone attack in broad daylight Tuesday on the center of Lviv, a historic city in western Ukraine that had been spared much of the fighting that has devastated other parts of the country.
More than a dozen people were reported injured, and the number of victims of the surprise attack was expected to rise, officials warned.
"There are already 13 injured people in hospitals in Lviv," Mayor Andriy Sadovyi posted online. "The number of victims is increasing."

In his evening address, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said that the Russians used Iranian-designed drones to attack Lviv and that one of them badly damaged the historic 17th century St. Andrew’s ​Church, which is a UNESCO heritage site.
"Iranian Shaheds, modernized by Russia, hit a church in Lviv — it's absolute perversion," Zelenskyy said.
The regional governor warned residents to stay off the streets.
Could the U.S. learn from Ukraine to fight Iran’s Shahed drones?
"The threat remains high," Maksym Kozytskyi posted online. "Stay in shelters!!!"
Prime Minister Yuliya Svyrydenko accused the Russians of deliberately going after civilians.
"Russia is attacking a crowded city centre ​in broad daylight," Svyrydenko posted.
Officials at the Russian Defense Ministry did not immediately respond to a request for comment on the news.
With a population of more than 700,000, Lviv, just a little more than 40 miles from the Polish border, is the biggest city in western Ukraine.
It is also the hometown of the famed Polish science fiction writer Stanislaw Lem, who imagined drone warfare in his 1964 novel "The Invincible."

Lviv was one of nearly a dozen regions across Ukraine that were hit by one of Russia's biggest aerial barrages since the start of the war, with more than 930 drone strikes and 34 missile attacks on other cities and key infrastructure, according to the Ukrainian air force.
Among other things, apartment buildings were hit, civilian infrastructure was damaged and even a train was targeted, it said.
Dozens of people were injured, including children, it said. Several deaths were also reported.
"The scale of this attack is clear evidence that Russia has no intent to end this war," Zelenskyy said.

title: Iranians grieving over school missile strike express rage at U.S.
summary: Zahra Monazzah’s son Soheil was at an elementary school in Minab, in southern Iran, on Feb. 28 when a missile struck the building, killing him and dozens of other children.
story: Zahra Monazzah’s son Soheil was at an elementary school in Minab, in southern Iran, on Feb. 28 when a missile struck the building, killing him and dozens of other children.

When she learned that the U.S. was most likely behind the school attack — possibly on the basis of outdated intelligence, an American official told NBC News two weeks ago — Monazzah and other families and neighbors said they were enraged at President Donald Trump.
“Trump should not think that killing our children has made us despair,” Monazzah said. “He should cry for himself, because he will end up in hell.”
Soheil died a "martyr," his mother said, two days before his eighth birthday. “The blood of our young people has always been shed by America, so this was not unexpected.”
Jafar Qasemi, one of the first responders on the scene, helped retrieve the bodies of the schoolchildren.

“My feeling is a mix of anger about this attack and a strong determination to defend ourselves,” he said. “This war is a test of honor and the defense of human dignity. Every drop of blood spilled in this path will make the spirit of resistance stronger.”
He is still traumatized by what he saw that day, he said.

“Most of them were children. No one spoke at first. It was like being in a silent film. Even a child sitting by the school wall whose face was completely burned,” he said, adding, “For me, time was moving very slowly.”
Qasemi said he opened a child’s bag that was covered in blood and was struck that the child had not had the chance to eat their snack for school that day.
“I still haven’t been able to come to terms with it. I can still smell blood and gunpowder, and the images of that day pass by me constantly,” he said.

title: As Trump touts progress in Iran peace talks, a new go-between gains influence
summary: Pakistan is joining a growing list of countries acting as go-betweens for the United States and Iran, four sources told NBC News, with two of those sources saying an in-person meeting could be held in the coming days in Islamabad
story: Pakistan is joining a growing list of countries acting as go-betweens for the United States and Iran, four sources told NBC News, with two of those sources saying an in-person meeting could be held in the coming days in Islamabad.

A diplomatic source said Pakistan is in conversations with both the U.S. and Iran and is “well poised to play an active role” in discussions to end the war, which has killed more than 2,000 people and displaced millions as it enters its fourth week. A Gulf official said Pakistan had been passing messages between the two countries for the past two days.
